channel.title: Graph Neural Networks on Benoit Gaüzère website
channel.description: Recent content in Graph Neural Networks on Benoit Gaüzère website

item.title: Spectral vs. Spatial Graph Convolutions in Practice
item.description: This post revisits a simple idea from our ICLR 2021 paper: most graph convolutions can be understood as filters on graph signals, and filters on graph can be reimplemented as convolution operators. This point of view helps connect spectral and spatial GNNs, and explains why many popular models behave similarly. Historically, graph convolutions were defined either in the spectral domain (via the Laplacian) or in the spatial domain (via neighborhood aggregation).
